commands.repair_reason: - [P2] The remaining action is maintainer acceptance of the compatibility-sensitive CLI output semantics, not an automated code repair.; Cleared: The diff only changes in-repo TypeScript row-normalization logic and a unit test, with no dependency, workflow, credential, or code-execution surface. (sha=b5390f5c1587b929caab4e6694d03a99ee57b552)
commands.review_summary:
The PR changes model-list row normalization so local-baseUrl models are treated as available even when registry availability omits the exact local model key, with a focused regression test.

PR surface: Source +2, Tests +18. Total +20 across 2 files.

Reproducibility: yes. at source level: current main computes `local` but still derives `available` from registry availability or provider-auth fallback, so a local row omitted from `availableKeys` can render unavailable. I did not run a live Ollama daemon in this read-only review, but the linked issue and PR body provide matching CLI context.
commands.review_followup: - [P2] The remaining action is maintainer acceptance of the compatibility-sensitive CLI output semantics, not an automated code repair.

target.title: fix(models): mark local Ollama rows available
target.body:
Closes #92224

## What Problem This Solves

Fixes an issue where users running `openclaw models list --json` could see a reachable local Ollama model reported with `local: true` but `available: false` when registry model-level availability did not include that exact model key.

## Why This Change Was Made

Local model rows already classify loopback/local `baseUrl` values, and the models CLI documents availability/auth as provider-level metadata from local-provider markers rather than a live per-model execution probe. This change treats local provider rows as available from that local marker while preserving registry model-key availability and provider-auth fallback for non-local rows.

## User Impact

Users with local Ollama models now get model-list output that matches the local-provider auth semantics: configured local Ollama rows no longer look unavailable solely because registry discovery omitted the exact model key.

## Evidence

- Claim proved: local Ollama-style rows stay `available: true` when `availableKeys` is present but missing the exact local model key.
- Commands / artifacts:
  - `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/commands/models/list.model-row.test.ts --reporter=verbose` -> passed, 4 tests.
  - `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/commands/models/list.rows.test.ts --reporter=verbose` -> passed, 4 tests.
  - `git diff --check` -> passed.
  - `python .agents\skills\autoreview\scripts\autoreview --mode local` -> clean, no accepted/actionable findings.
  - `node --import tsx src/entry.ts models list --json --provider ollama` at commit `b5390f5c15`, with isolated `OPENCLAW_HOME`, isolated `OPENCLAW_CONFIG_PATH`, and a local loopback Ollama-compatible API endpoint -> passed.
- Observed result: the new regression test fails before the fix with `expected false to be true`, then passes after local base URL rows are included in row availability.
- Loopback CLI proof output:

```text
COMMAND: node --import tsx src/entry.ts models list --json --provider ollama
COMMIT: b5390f5c15
OPENCLAW_HOME: home (isolated temp dir)
OPENCLAW_CONFIG_PATH: openclaw.json (isolated temp file)
MOCK OLLAMA REQUESTS: GET /api/tags, POST /api/show
EXIT: 0
```

```json
{
  "count": 1,
  "models": [
    {
      "key": "ollama/qwen3.6:35b-a3b",
      "name": "qwen3.6:35b-a3b",
      "input": "text",
      "contextWindow": 131072,
      "local": true,
      "available": true,
      "tags": [
        "default"
      ],
      "missing": false
    }
  ]
}
```

- Not tested / proof gaps: I could not run against a real Ollama daemon on this Windows host: `curl http://127.0.0.1:11434/api/tags` failed to connect, no `ollama` CLI is installed, and Docker Desktop's daemon is not running. The added CLI proof exercises the real OpenClaw CLI entrypoint, config loading, provider discovery HTTP calls, and JSON output against a loopback Ollama-compatible endpoint, but it is not a full real-daemon Ollama proof.
target.author: qingminglong

commands.reason: PR is not opted into ClawSweeper autofix or automerge
